description:
Antagonistic quality review of `hx-tile` (T3-04) **after implementation completes**.

Review every file in `packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-tile/` critically.

**Audit areas:**
1. TypeScript — strict, clickable/selectable prop typed, selected state typed, variant typed
2. Accessibility — if clickable: keyboard activatable, role appropriate (button or link), if selectable: aria-selected or aria-checked, focus visible, axe-core pass
3. Tests — clickable vs static, selectable state, hx-select event, keyboard activation, 80%+ coverage
4. Storybook — static, clickable, selectable variants
5. CSS — `--hx-*` tokens, selected state visual indicator, focus ring, CSS parts (tile, header, body, footer)
6. Performance — bundle < 5KB
7. Drupal — Twig-renderable

**Output:** Write findings to `packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-tile/AUDIT.md` with severity (P0/P1/P2). DO NOT fix — document only. Commit AUDIT.md as your PR.
title: AUDIT: hx-tile — T3-04 antagonistic quality review

summary:
## Summary: Antagonistic Quality Review of `hx-tile` (T3-04)

### Changes Implemented
- Performed comprehensive antagonistic audit of all 5 hx-tile source files
- Wrote findings to `packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-tile/AUDIT.md` with severity classifications
- Committed and pushed AUDIT.md to branch

### Files Modified
- `packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-tile/AUDIT.md` (created, 324 lines)

### Findings Summary

**1 P0 (Critical/Blocker)**
- P0-01: No static/non-interactive mode — every enabled tile unconditionally gets `role="button"`, `aria-pressed`, click/keyboard handlers. Cannot create decorative tiles.

**9 P1 (High Severity)**
- P1-01: CSS parts mismatch — spec requires `tile, header, body, footer`; component exports `base, icon, label, description, badge`
- P1-02: No `prefers-reduced-motion` media query — uses transition + transform animations in a healthcare context
- P1-03: Selected state uses raw semantic tokens directly, missing `--hx-tile-selected-border-color` / `--hx-tile-selected-bg` component tokens
- P1-04: `aria-pressed` applied to all non-link tiles regardless of intent — semantic mismatch for navigation-intent tiles
- P1-05: Disabled-click test uses `el.click()` on host, not shadow `[part="base"]` — unreliable test
- P1-06: No keyboard test for disabled state (Enter/Space on disabled tile)
- P1-07: No test for "static vs clickable" distinction (mirrors P0-01 gap)
- P1-08: No Drupal behavior file — `hx-click` event intercepts native navigation with no consumer guidance
- P1-09: `selected` attribute reflects on host in link mode but visual state is silently suppressed

**13 P2 (Medium Severity)**
- Duplicate test assertion, debug `page.screenshot()` artifacts in axe tests, Space key on link tiles fires `hx-click` (undocumented), hardcoded hex colors in Storybook story, NavigationGrid story misuses selectable tiles for navigation, meta render empty href, `:host` inline-block vs block mismatch, no `aria-label` support, incomplete Storybook play functions, missing Twig template, misleading variable name, missing explicit `tabindex="-1"` on disabled links, hardcoded hex fallbacks in theme tokens

### Risks/Blockers Encountered
- None. All source files were readable and the audit was straightforward.

### Learnings
- The hx-tile component is fundamentally missing a "static" mode — this is a design-level gap that needs resolution before the component can be considered spec-complete
- The CSS parts API doesn't match the feature spec at all (base/icon/label vs tile/header/body/footer) — implementation deviated from spec
- The `prefers-reduced-motion` gap is a recurring pattern likely affecting other animated components in the library

### Notes for Developer
- The P0 issue (no static mode) requires an API design decision before implementation: likely a `clickable` boolean prop or an explicit `mode` enum
- P1-01 (CSS parts) may require a spec update rather than a component change — verify the intended API before refactoring
- P1-02 (prefers-reduced-motion) is a quick CSS fix but should be applied library-wide as a pattern

### Verification Status
- Playwright verification was not applicable to this task — the output is a documentation file (AUDIT.md), not a functional implementation.
- `npm run verify` passed (0 errors, 0 warnings) before commit.
- Git diff confirms only `AUDIT.md` was created; no source files were modified.
